senses.definition: past tense of prime, meaning to prepare or prepare something with prime or to make something ready with a prime action.
examples: He primmed the pump before starting the engine.
The technicians primmed the system to ensure smooth operation.

senses.definition: a person or thing that primes; something that prepares or prefaces something else.
examples: The primer is the first coat that acts as a primmer for paint.
She is the primmer of the discussion, setting the stage.

senses.definition: plural of primmer; multiple things that prepare or precede something.
examples: The project has several primmers that need attention.
These primmers help set up the main presentation.

senses.definition: most prime or most perfect; having the highest level of primeness.
examples: That is the primmest illustration of efficiency.
They wore primmest expressions during the ceremony.

senses.definition: present participle of prime; to prepare or coat with primer.
examples: He is primming the walls before painting.
The device is primming itself for operation.

senses.definition: the quality of being prim; showing proper decorum, formality, or being overly proper.
examples: Her primness made the room feel stiff.
A streak of primness runs through the etiquette manual.

senses.definition: plural of primness; instances or degrees of being prim.
examples: Various primnesses were noted in the etiquette review.
Different primnesses appeared across cultures.

senses.definition: a first-class or excellent person or thing; something prime or best in its class.
examples: That wine is primo.
He is primo among his peers.

senses.definition: a female who is the firstborn child or the female parent of a breed or lineage; archaic/rare term in some contexts.
examples: Literary texts sometimes use primogenetrix to denote a female firstborn.

senses.definition: of or relating to the earliest or first of a kind; primitive or original.
examples: Primogenial instincts guided early humans.
The primogenial state of the universe is debated by scientists.

senses.definition: root form relating to birth or origin in some contexts; often appears in compounds like primogenital.
examples: In the word family, primogenit relates to birth order.

senses.definition: relating to the first generation or to birth organs in a stylized or archaic sense; often used in biological or literary contexts.
examples: Primogenital traits are studied in evolutionary biology.

senses.definition: relating to primogeniture or the rights of the firstborn.
examples: The primogenitary law affects inheritance in some cultures.

senses.definition: describing something that relates to being the first of its kind; original or primary.
examples: The primogenitive traits shaped the lineage.

senses.definition: plural form referring to primary or firstborn characteristics or elements.
examples: The primigenitives influenced succession rules.

senses.definition: the first ancestor or the originator of a line; the founder.
examples: He was regarded as the primogenitor of the clan.

senses.definition: plural of primogenitor; multiple founders or originators.
examples: The primogenitors of the city are celebrated in legends.

senses.definition: plural feminine form referring to female primogenitors; female founders or originators.
examples: The primogenitrices are celebrated in genealogical histories.

senses.definition: a female primogenitor; the first female ancestor or originator.
examples: The primogenitrix appears in ancient genealogies.

senses.definition: plural of primogenitrix; multiple female primogenitors.
examples: The primogenitrixes are recorded in royal genealogies.

senses.definition: alternate spelling variant of primogenitrices; plural feminine originators.
examples: The primogenitrixes guided the lineage.

senses.definition: plural or truncated form referring to firstborn individuals or origins; used in scholarly or archaic texts.
examples: The primogenits of the tribe are honored.

senses.definition: the right of the firstborn child to inherit the parent's estate; a system of inheritance by primogeniture.
examples: In many monarchies, primogeniture determined succession.
The estate passed by primogeniture to the eldest son.

senses.definition: plural of primogeniture; systems or instances of inheritance by the firstborn.
examples: Different cultures had various primogenitures over time.

senses.definition: the state or condition of being governed by primogeniture; the position of inheriting by the firstborn.
examples: The primogenitureship shaped dynastic politics.

senses.definition: forming or producing at an early stage; primordial.
examples: Primogenous materials formed the basis of the proto-synthesis.
